key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: Q1 2026 Distributable Earnings per share $0.24 (SEVN-Q1-2026-Earnings-Presentation.pdf, p.5) annualized x4

Rights-offering capital (cash + unfunded commitments) redeploys over roughly two years back to the $0.28/quarter run rate, then grows slowly with the floating-rate book; no realized credit losses; discount to book narrows modestly as the sector re-rates toward ~0.75-0.85x book (about 9x distributable earnings).

open_questions: Confirm the base case redeployment timeline (roughly two years to the $0.28 per share run rate) against the next one to two quarters of actual results
Approve or reject promoting the sector price-to-book discount theme in `context_overlay` beyond context-only status
Review the `stance_proposal.override_reason` in `valuation.json` and the thin total synthesis IRR path before treating 16.04% as anything more than directional
No third-party research is indexed yet; revisit after `scan_third_party_sources.py SEVN` picks up sell-side or Substack coverage
thesis: Seven Hills Realty Trust is a commercial mortgage real estate investment trust that originates and holds first mortgage loans on middle-market transitional commercial properties across the United States. **[Fact]** As of March 31, 2026 it held 26 floating-rate loans with total commitments of $776.0 million, an average loan size of $30.0 million, financed with secured repurchase facilities from four banks. **[Fact]** The trust is externally managed by Tremont Realty Capital, a wholly owned subsidiary of The RMR Group, which also owns roughly 20% of SEVN's shares. **[Fact]** SEVN pays Tremont a base management fee of 1.5% of equity per year plus an incentive fee equal to 20% of core earnings above a 7% annual hurdle. **[Fact]**
why_market_wrong: SEVN trades at $8.40 per share, or about 0.58 times its $14.47 book value per share, a discount typical of commercial mortgage REITs during a period of broad worry about office and other transitional commercial real estate credit. **[Fact]** SEVN's own loan book does not show that stress: no realized losses through the first quarter of 2026, a weighted average loan-to-value ratio of 66%, a weighted average risk rating of 2.8 out of 5, and no loans secured by office property in central business districts. **[Fact]** In December 2025 the trust raised $65.2 million of gross proceeds in a rights offering, and as of March 31, 2026 it still held $56.6 million of cash and $44.0 million of unfunded loan commitments waiting to be invested at a current weighted average yield near 7.8%. **[Fact]** Distributable earnings per share fell to $0.24 in the first quarter of 2026, from $0.28 in the fourth quarter of 2025, purely because that new capital has not finished earning a full quarter of income yet. **[Inference]** The market appears to be pricing the temporary earnings dip and sector-wide office fear together, rather than SEVN's own underwriting.
